title: 6.1 Trophic Levels
description: Trophic levels — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: What is the ultimate source of energy in living organisms?
Which organisms are the first to trap energy from the sun and produce their own food?
Producers are also known as which of the following?
What does the term 'autotroph' mean?
Animals that feed on either plants or other animals as a source of energy are known as what?
Consumers are also known by which term?
What do you call an animal that feeds on plants only?
What do you call an animal that feeds on other animals only?
What do you call an animal that feeds on both plants and animals?
The process by which energy is transferred from plants through a chain of organisms is known as what?
options: The food chain or food web
questions.correctAnswer: The food chain or food web

questions.question: What does a food chain represent?
options: Nutritive interaction among biotic components of the ecosystem
questions.correctAnswer: Nutritive interaction among biotic components of the ecosystem

questions.question: What type of energy flow occurs in a food chain?
From where to where does energy flow in a food chain?
options: From the sun to producers and then to consumers
questions.correctAnswer: From the sun to producers and then to consumers

questions.question: Where are plants (producers) positioned in a food chain?
What is a trophic level?
options: The amount of energy in an organism
The position of an organism in an order of receiving energy
The type of food an organism eats
questions.correctAnswer: The position of an organism in an order of receiving energy

questions.question: How many trophic levels do food chains usually range from?
Which trophic level do plants (producers) occupy?
Which trophic level do herbivores occupy?
Which trophic level do carnivores that eat herbivores occupy?
What is a feeding level?
options: The amount of food an organism eats
The order of consuming or eating one another
The type of food available in an ecosystem
questions.correctAnswer: The order of consuming or eating one another

questions.question: At which feeding level are herbivores (primary consumers)?
At which feeding level are carnivores that eat herbivores?
An omnivorous animal occupies how many positions in the food chain?
What is a food web?
options: A single path of energy flow
An interconnected food chain that makes interwoven or web-like structures
questions.correctAnswer: An interconnected food chain that makes interwoven or web-like structures

questions.question: What type of energy flow does a food web exhibit?
How does a food chain differ from a food web in terms of paths?
options: Food chain follows multiple paths, food web follows a single path
Food chain follows a single path, food web follows multiple paths
questions.correctAnswer: Food chain follows a single path, food web follows multiple paths

questions.question: Which of the following represents a feeding relationship that actually exists in an ecosystem?
What is the first trophic level organism in Figure 6.2 of the textbook?
What is the second trophic level organism in Figure 6.2 of the textbook?
What is the third trophic level organism in Figure 6.2 of the textbook?
In Figure 6.1, what does the letter P stand for?
In Figure 6.1, what does the letter H stand for?
In Figure 6.1, what does C1 stand for?
In Figure 6.1, what does C2 stand for?
